---
title: "Chapter 5 - Sannyasa Yoga"
---

<div class="chapter-title-sanskrit">संन्यासयोगः</div>

<nav class="chapter-nav"><a href="chapter-04.html">Previous chapter</a><a href="chapter-06.html">Next chapter</a></nav>

<div class="verse-list">
<article class="verse" id="v-5-1" data-reference="5.1">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-1">5.1</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." aria-label="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." title="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." data-glossary-id="krishna">Krishna</span>, you praise the renunciation of actions, and again <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>; tell me for certain which of the two is better.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">संन्यासं कर्मणां कृष्ण पुनर्योगं च शंससि ।<br>
यच्छ्रेय एतयोरेकं तन्मे ब्रूहि सुनिश्चितम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-2" data-reference="5.2">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-2">5.2</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Both renunciation and the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span> of action lead to the highest good; but of the two, the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span> of action excels the renunciation of action.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">संन्यासः कर्मयोगश्च निःश्रेयसकरावुभौ ।<br>
तयोस्तु कर्मसंन्यासात्कर्मयोगो विशिष्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-3" data-reference="5.3">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-3">5.3</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Know him to be a constant renunciant who neither hates nor desires; free of the pairs of opposites, O mighty-armed one, he is released from bondage with ease.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ज्ञेयः स नित्यसंन्यासी यो न द्वेष्टि न काङ्क्षति ।<br>
निर्द्वन्द्वो हि महाबाहो सुखं बन्धात्प्रमुच्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-4" data-reference="5.4">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-4">5.4</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">It is the childish, not the wise, who speak of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Sankhya: A path or framework of discriminative knowledge; here not always a formal school label." aria-label="Sankhya: A path or framework of discriminative knowledge; here not always a formal school label." title="Sankhya: A path or framework of discriminative knowledge; here not always a formal school label." data-glossary-id="sankhya">Sankhya</span> and <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span> as different; one rightly established in even one of them gains the fruit of both.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">साङ्ख्ययोगौ पृथग्बालाः प्रवदन्ति न पण्डिताः ।<br>
एकमप्यास्थितः सम्यगुभयोर्विन्दते फलम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-5" data-reference="5.5">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-5">5.5</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The state reached by <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Sankhya: A path or framework of discriminative knowledge; here not always a formal school label." aria-label="Sankhya: A path or framework of discriminative knowledge; here not always a formal school label." title="Sankhya: A path or framework of discriminative knowledge; here not always a formal school label." data-glossary-id="sankhya">Sankhya</span> is reached by <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span> as well;<br>
he who sees <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Sankhya: A path or framework of discriminative knowledge; here not always a formal school label." aria-label="Sankhya: A path or framework of discriminative knowledge; here not always a formal school label." title="Sankhya: A path or framework of discriminative knowledge; here not always a formal school label." data-glossary-id="sankhya">Sankhya</span> and <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span> as one truly sees.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यत्साङ्ख्यैः प्राप्यते स्थानं तद्योगैरपि गम्यते ।<br>
एकं साङ्ख्यं च योगं च यः पश्यति स पश्यति ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-6" data-reference="5.6">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-6">5.6</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">But renunciation is hard to attain without <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>, O mighty-armed one; the sage yoked in <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span> soon reaches <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">संन्यासस्तु महाबाहो दुःखमाप्तुमयोगतः ।<br>
योगयुक्तो मुनिर्ब्रह्म नचिरेणाधिगच्छति ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-7" data-reference="5.7">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-7">5.7</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Yoked in <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>, his self purified, self-mastered, his senses subdued, his self become the self of all beings—he is not tainted even while acting.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">योगयुक्तो विशुद्धात्मा विजितात्मा जितेन्द्रियः ।<br>
सर्वभूतात्मभूतात्मा कुर्वन्नपि न लिप्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-8" data-reference="5.8">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-8">5.8</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">&quot;I do nothing at all,&quot; the disciplined knower of truth would hold, even while seeing, hearing, touching, smelling, eating, moving, sleeping, breathing,</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">नैव किञ्चित्करोमीति युक्तो मन्येत तत्त्ववित् ।<br>
पश्यञ्श‍ृण्वन्स्पृशञ्जिघ्रन्नश्नन्गच्छन्स्वपञ्श्वसन् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-9" data-reference="5.9">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-9">5.9</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">speaking, releasing, grasping, opening and closing the eyes—convinced that it is only the senses moving among the objects of sense.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">प्रलपन्विसृजन्गृह्णन्नुन्मिषन्निमिषन्नपि ।<br>
इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेषु वर्तन्त इति धारयन् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-10" data-reference="5.10">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-10">5.10</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">One who acts, offering his actions to <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span> and abandoning attachment, is untouched by sin, as a lotus leaf by water.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ब्रह्मण्याधाय कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा करोति यः ।<br>
लिप्यते न स पापेन पद्मपत्रमिवाम्भसा ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-11" data-reference="5.11">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-11">5.11</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">With body, mind, understanding, and the senses alone, the yogins act, abandoning attachment, for the purifying of the self.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">कायेन मनसा बुद्ध्या केवलैरिन्द्रियैरपि ।<br>
योगिनः कर्म कुर्वन्ति सङ्गं त्यक्त्वात्मशुद्धये ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-12" data-reference="5.12">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-12">5.12</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The disciplined one, abandoning the fruit of action, attains lasting peace; the undisciplined, attached to the fruit through desire, is bound.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">युक्तः कर्मफलं त्यक्त्वा शान्तिमाप्नोति नैष्ठिकीम् ।<br>
अयुक्तः कामकारेण फले सक्तो निबध्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-13" data-reference="5.13">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-13">5.13</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Renouncing all actions with the mind, the embodied one rests at ease, in command, in the city of nine gates, neither acting nor causing action.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सर्वकर्माणि मनसा संन्यस्यास्ते सुखं वशी ।<br>
नवद्वारे पुरे देही नैव कुर्वन्न कारयन् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-14" data-reference="5.14">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-14">5.14</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The Lord creates for the world neither agency nor actions, nor the joining of action to its fruit; it is inherent nature that unfolds.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">न कर्तृत्वं न कर्माणि लोकस्य सृजति प्रभुः ।<br>
न कर्मफलसंयोगं स्वभावस्तु प्रवर्तते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-15" data-reference="5.15">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-15">5.15</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The all-pervading Lord takes on no one&#x27;s sin or merit; knowledge is veiled by ignorance, and by this beings are deluded.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">नादत्ते कस्यचित्पापं न चैव सुकृतं विभुः ।<br>
अज्ञानेनावृतं ज्ञानं तेन मुह्यन्ति जन्तवः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-16" data-reference="5.16">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-16">5.16</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">But for those in whom that ignorance is destroyed by knowledge of the self, knowledge, like the sun, lights up the Supreme.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ज्ञानेन तु तदज्ञानं येषां नाशितमात्मनः ।<br>
तेषामादित्यवज्ज्ञानं प्रकाशयति तत्परम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-17" data-reference="5.17">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-17">5.17</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">With understanding fixed on it, their self set on it, devoted to it, intent on it as their goal, they go where there is no return, their faults washed away by knowledge.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तद्बुद्धयस्तदात्मानस्तन्निष्ठास्तत्परायणाः ।<br>
गच्छन्त्यपुनरावृत्तिं ज्ञाननिर्धूतकल्मषाः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-18" data-reference="5.18">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-18">5.18</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">On a learned and humble <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahmin: A member of the priestly or learned class; Sanskrit brāhmaṇa." aria-label="Brahmin: A member of the priestly or learned class; Sanskrit brāhmaṇa." title="Brahmin: A member of the priestly or learned class; Sanskrit brāhmaṇa." data-glossary-id="brahmin">brahmin</span>, on a cow, an elephant, a dog, and an outcaste, the wise look with an equal eye.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">विद्याविनयसम्पन्ने ब्राह्मणे गवि हस्तिनि ।<br>
शुनि चैव श्वपाके च पण्डिताः समदर्शिनः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-19" data-reference="5.19">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-19">5.19</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Even here rebirth is overcome by those whose minds rest in equanimity; for <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span> is flawless and the same in all, and so they are established in <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">इहैव तैर्जितः सर्गो येषां साम्ये स्थितं मनः ।<br>
निर्दोषं हि समं ब्रह्म तस्माद् ब्रह्मणि ते स्थिताः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-20" data-reference="5.20">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-20">5.20</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Neither rejoicing at the pleasant nor recoiling at the unpleasant, steady of understanding and unbewildered, the knower of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span> is established in <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">न प्रहृष्येत्प्रियं प्राप्य नोद्विजेत्प्राप्य चाप्रियम् ।<br>
स्थिरबुद्धिरसम्मूढो ब्रह्मविद् ब्रह्मणि स्थितः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-21" data-reference="5.21">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-21">5.21</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">With the self unattached to outward contacts, he finds the happiness that is in the self;<br>
his self yoked in the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span> of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span>, he attains unending happiness.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">बाह्यस्पर्शेष्वसक्तात्मा विन्दत्यात्मनि यत्सुखम् ।<br>
स ब्रह्मयोगयुक्तात्मा सुखमक्षयमश्नुते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-22" data-reference="5.22">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-22">5.22</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">For the pleasures born of contact are wombs of suffering; they have a beginning and an end, O son of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." aria-label="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." title="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." data-glossary-id="kaunteya">Kunti</span>, and the wise take no delight in them.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ये हि संस्पर्शजा भोगा दुःखयोनय एव ते ।<br>
आद्यन्तवन्तः कौन्तेय न तेषु रमते बुधः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-23" data-reference="5.23">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-23">5.23</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">He who can withstand, even here, before he is freed from the body, the force born of desire and anger—he is disciplined, he is a happy man.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">शक्नोतीहैव यः सोढुं प्राक्शरीरविमोक्षणात् ।<br>
कामक्रोधोद्भवं वेगं स युक्तः स सुखी नरः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-24" data-reference="5.24">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-24">5.24</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">He whose happiness is within, whose delight is within, whose light too is within—that yogin, having become <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span>, attains the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="nirvana: Extinction, peace, or release; here often linked with Brahman." aria-label="nirvana: Extinction, peace, or release; here often linked with Brahman." title="nirvana: Extinction, peace, or release; here often linked with Brahman." data-glossary-id="nirvana">nirvana</span> of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">योऽन्तःसुखोऽन्तरारामस्तथान्तर्ज्योतिरेव यः ।<br>
स योगी ब्रह्मनिर्वाणं ब्रह्मभूतोऽधिगच्छति ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-25" data-reference="5.25">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-25">5.25</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The seers whose sins are spent, whose doubts are cut away, who are self-mastered and rejoice in the welfare of all beings, attain the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="nirvana: Extinction, peace, or release; here often linked with Brahman." aria-label="nirvana: Extinction, peace, or release; here often linked with Brahman." title="nirvana: Extinction, peace, or release; here often linked with Brahman." data-glossary-id="nirvana">nirvana</span> of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">लभन्ते ब्रह्मनिर्वाणमृषयः क्षीणकल्मषाः ।<br>
छिन्नद्वैधा यतात्मानः सर्वभूतहिते रताः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-26" data-reference="5.26">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-26">5.26</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">For ascetics freed from desire and anger, their minds controlled, who have known the self, the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="nirvana: Extinction, peace, or release; here often linked with Brahman." aria-label="nirvana: Extinction, peace, or release; here often linked with Brahman." title="nirvana: Extinction, peace, or release; here often linked with Brahman." data-glossary-id="nirvana">nirvana</span> of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span> lies near on every side.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">कामक्रोधवियुक्तानां यतीनां यतचेतसाम् ।<br>
अभितो ब्रह्मनिर्वाणं वर्तते विदितात्मनाम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-27" data-reference="5.27">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-27">5.27</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Shutting out the outward contacts, fixing the gaze between the brows, making the inward and outward breaths even as they move through the nostrils,</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">स्पर्शान्कृत्वा बहिर्बाह्यांश्चक्षुश्चैवान्तरे भ्रुवोः ।<br>
प्राणापानौ समौ कृत्वा नासाभ्यन्तरचारिणौ ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-28" data-reference="5.28">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-28">5.28</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">the sage who has mastered his senses, mind, and understanding, intent on liberation, freed of desire, fear, and anger, is forever free.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यतेन्द्रियमनोबुद्धिर्मुनिर्मोक्षपरायणः ।<br>
विगतेच्छाभयक्रोधो यः सदा मुक्त एव सः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-29" data-reference="5.29">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-29">5.29</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Knowing me as the enjoyer of sacrifices and austerities, the great lord of all the worlds, the friend of every being, he attains peace.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">भोक्तारं यज्ञतपसां सर्वलोकमहेश्वरम् ।<br>
सुहृदं सर्वभूतानां ज्ञात्वा मां शान्तिमृच्छति ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

</div>
